assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Step 4 Root Cause Analysis explicitly identifies two independent root causes in a summary table: '1 | Convention reference formatter applies incorrect case transform (lowercase + kebab-case) instead of preserving title case | shared/convention-utils.md' and '2 | Task creation uses Feature issue type ID (10142) instead of Task issue type ID (10050) | plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a'. Steps 2-3 trace both code paths in detail. Step 4 includes detailed 'What/Why/Where/How to verify' sections for each root cause.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Step 4 states: 'These two root causes are independent: They affect different modules (shared/convention-utils.md vs plan-feature/SKILL.md). They involve different code paths (heading formatting vs Jira issue creation). ... This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6).' decomposition-guard.md confirms: 'These root causes are in different modules (shared/convention-utils.md vs plan-feature/SKILL.md) and involve independent code paths (heading formatting vs Jira issue creation).'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents both issues with affected files: '1. Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and '2. Task creation uses wrong issue type ID (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'. Each issue includes a description, example, and affected scope.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md explicitly offers two options: '1. Proceed -- create a single Task covering all fixes' and '2. Split -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually'. It then prompts 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends with a prompt 'Choose (1/2):' and an explicit note: 'This skill will NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes. User input is required before proceeding.' No task creation output exists in the outputs directory, confirming the skill stopped and waited for user input.
